entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being evaluated for resistance to downy mildew

entities.name: PERONOSCLEROSPORA SORGHI
entities.description: The causal agent of downy mildew in sorghum

entities.name: INOCULATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used for the artificial promotion of downy mildew

entities.name: SPROUTED SEEDS INCUBATED BETWEEN INFECTED LEAVES
entities.description: An inoculation technique using sporulating infected leaves

entities.name: SPROUTED SEEDS DIPPED IN CONIDIAL SUSPENSION
entities.description: An inoculation technique involving dipping sprouted seeds in conidial suspension

entities.name: SPROUTED SEEDS SPRAYED WITH CONIDIAL SUSPENSION
entities.description: An inoculation technique involving spraying sprouted seeds with conidial suspension

entities.name: SEEDLINGS AT PLUMULE STAGE INOCULATED WITH DROPS
entities.description: An inoculation technique using drops of conidial suspension on plumule stage seedlings

entities.name: SEEDLINGS AT PLUMULE STAGE SPRAYED WITH CONIDIAL SUSPENSION
entities.description: An inoculation technique spraying conidial suspension on plumule stage seedlings

entities.name: SEEDLINGS SHOWERED WITH CONIDIA
entities.description: An inoculation technique where seedlings are showered with conidia from infected leaves

entities.name: SPRAYING SEEDLINGS AT ONE-LEAF STAGE WITH CONIDIAL SUSPENSION
entities.description: The most effective inoculation technique showing highest systemic infection

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum genotypes to withstand downy mildew infection

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where inoculation techniques were evaluated

entities.name: FIELD SCREENINGS
entities.description: The process of testing genotypes for resistance in field conditions

entities.name: SYSTEMIC INFECTION
entities.description: The spread of the pathogen throughout the plant tissue
